name: Maturin (Build Rust Extensions for Python)

performance.notes: Build tool performance; resulting Rust extensions have near-native speed

agent_readiness.notes: Build and publish Rust-based Python extensions. Works with PyO3 for Python bindings. maturin develop (local dev), maturin build (wheel), maturin publish (PyPI). Supports mixed Rust/Python projects. Zig cross-compilation for wheels. GitHub Actions integration. `maturin new` scaffolding. PEP 517/518 compatible. Used by: polars, ruff, pydantic-core, cryptography, orjson, tiktoken. The standard way to ship Rust as Python packages.

gotchas: Requires Rust toolchain on developer machines
Cross-compilation for multiple platforms needs zig or CI matrix
PyO3 binding patterns have learning curve
ABI compatibility requires careful Python version targeting
best_when: Python packages needing Rust performance (data processing, parsing, crypto)
Publishing Rust code as Python wheels on PyPI
Replacing slow Python C extensions with safer Rust code
avoid_when: Simple performance needs solvable with NumPy/Cython
summary: Maturin is the build tool for Rust-based Python extensions (with PyO3). maturin develop, build, publish. PEP 517/518 compatible. Zig cross-compilation for wheels. Used by polars, ruff, pydantic-core, orjson, tiktoken. The standard way to ship Rust as Python packages. New to Python-Rust interop? Start with PyO3 docs, then maturin for packaging.
